data.body:
## Bug Description

When orchestrators check `worktree.isolation_enabled` via the dso shim (`.claude/scripts/dso read-config worktree.isolation_enabled`), the shim fails silently with exit 127 ('command not found: read-config') if 'read-config' is not registered in the shim's command table (only 'read-config.sh' exists).

## What Happened in This Session

I used: `ISOLATION_ENABLED=$(.claude/scripts/dso read-config worktree.isolation_enabled 2>/dev/null || true)`

The `2>/dev/null` suppressed the error message. The `|| true` suppressed the non-zero exit. The variable was assigned empty string with no visible indication of failure. Step 2 of worktree-dispatch.md treats empty as 'isolation disabled', so 8 sub-agents ran in shared-directory mode despite `worktree.isolation_enabled=true` in config.

## My Rationalization (why I didn't catch it)

I checked the value, got empty, concluded isolation was off, and rationalized that since sub-agents write different files and don't commit, the impact was minimal. This was wrong: I should have validated the command succeeded before trusting an empty output.

## Correct Read Pattern

`bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/scripts/read-config.sh" worktree.isolation_enabled` works and returns 'true'. The shim path '.claude/scripts/dso read-config' (no .sh) fails because the shim doesn't dispatch that variant.

## Impact

8 sub-agents ran in shared-directory mode when they should have been isolated. No data loss (all wrote to different files, no commits made). Isolation guarantee was not honored.
